identity.short_name: CRT exponent targets with 2-adic and Hensel-lifted quartic residue coupling

problem_spec.summary: Construct a unit A by CRT modulo 2^m p^k q^2 ell from residues A \equiv u (mod 2^m), A \equiv 1+p (mod p^k), A \equiv g (mod q^2), A \equiv h (mod ell). Define a 2-adic target D \equiv u^t (mod 2^m), a Hensel-lifted quartic target B (the unique lift to q^2 of a chosen square root of -1 mod q), and an auxiliary prime target C \equiv h^{(ell-1)/2} (mod ell). Translate A^n congruences into a coupled exponent system with non-coprime moduli, solve by gcd reduction / sequential CRT, then normalize n0 modulo 999.
givens: $p,q,\ell$ are pairwise distinct odd primes, $k \ge 3$, and $q \equiv 1 \pmod 4$.
$g$ is a primitive root modulo $q^2$, hence $\operatorname{ord}_{q^2}(g)=q(q-1)$.
$h$ is a primitive root modulo $\ell$, hence $\operatorname{ord}_{\ell}(h)=\ell-1$.
$x_0$ is an integer with $x_0^2 \equiv -1 \pmod q$ and $q \nmid x_0$.
$D$ is the least nonnegative integer with $D \equiv u^{t} \pmod{2^m}$.
$B$ is the least nonnegative integer with $B \equiv x_0 \pmod q$ and $B^2 \equiv -1 \pmod{q^2}$.
$C$ is the least nonnegative integer with $C \equiv h^{(\ell-1)/2} \pmod{\ell}$.
$A$ is the least nonnegative integer with $A \equiv u \pmod{2^m}$, $A \equiv 1+p \pmod{p^k}$, $A \equiv g \pmod{q^2}$, and $A \equiv h \pmod{\ell}$.
ask: Determine the unique integer $N$ with $0 \le N < 999$ and $N \equiv n_0 \pmod{999}$, where $n_0$ is the least positive $n$ such that $A^n \equiv D \pmod{2^m}$, $A^n \equiv 1 \pmod{p^k}$, $A^n \equiv B \pmod{q^2}$, and $A^n \equiv C \pmod{\ell}$.

parameter(s).name: m
parameter(s).purpose: supplies a 2-adic modulus $2^m$ whose unit group has maximal cyclic component of size $2^{m-2}$

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: supplies the odd prime-power modulus $p^k$ and enables an LTE-based order computation for $1+p$

parameter(s).name: k
parameter(s).purpose: sets the odd prime-power depth and forces the exponent kernel $p^{k-1}$

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: supplies the prime-square modulus $q^2$ and allows a Hensel-lifted square root of $-1$

parameter(s).name: ell
parameter(s).purpose: supplies an auxiliary prime modulus creating a fourth exponent congruence modulo $\ell-1$

parameter(s).name: u
parameter(s).purpose: makes the 2-adic power constraint translate to the linear congruence $n \equiv t \pmod{2^{m-2}}$

parameter(s).name: g
parameter(s).purpose: rigidifies exponents modulo $q^2$ and enables order-$4$ target classification

parameter(s).name: t
parameter(s).purpose: target exponent for the 2-adic constraint $A^n \equiv u^t \pmod{2^m}$

parameter(s).name: x0
parameter(s).domain: integer root of $x^2 \equiv -1 \pmod q$ with $q \nmid x_0$
parameter(s).purpose: selects which of the two order-$4$ lifts is used in the $q^2$ constraint

invariant(s).name: CRT_synthesized_unit
invariant(s).statement: $M=2^m p^k q^2 \ell$ has pairwise coprime factors, so CRT gives a unique $A \pmod M$ from the four local residues.

invariant(s).name: hensel_quartic_lift_invariant
invariant(s).statement: If $x_0^2 \equiv -1 \pmod q$ and $q \nmid x_0$, then there is a unique $B \pmod{q^2}$ with $B \equiv x_0 \pmod q$ and $B^2 \equiv -1 \pmod{q^2}$; this $B$ has order $4$ modulo $q^2$.

invariant(s).name: coupling_gcd_compatibility
invariant(s).statement: The exponent system requires compatibility modulo shared gcds when combining $2^{m-2}$, $q(q-1)$, and $\ell-1$ via gcd reduction.

essential_assumption(s): $u$ must have maximal order $2^{m-2}$ modulo $2^m$ so the 2-adic condition becomes a single linear congruence in $n$.
$q \equiv 1 \pmod 4$ and $q \nmid x_0$ are required so $x^2 \equiv -1 \pmod q$ has a simple root that lifts uniquely to $q^2$.
$g$ being a primitive root modulo $q^2$ is required to avoid discrete logarithm ambiguity and to classify the order-$4$ target exponent uniquely once $x_0$ is fixed.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The four power congruences translate to the coupled exponent system $n \equiv t \pmod{2^{m-2}}$, $n \equiv 0 \pmod{p^{k-1}}$, $n \equiv r \pmod{q(q-1)}$, $n \equiv (\ell-1)/2 \pmod{\ell-1}$, where $r$ is uniquely forced by the chosen Hensel lift (pinned by $x_0$). Solving by gcd reduction and sequential CRT yields a single residue class, hence a unique least positive $n_0$ and a unique $N \equiv n_0 \pmod{999}$ with $0 \le N < 999$.

technique(s).name: Chinese_remainder
technique(s).purpose: construct the unique CRT-synthesized unit $A$ modulo $2^m p^k q^2 \ell$

technique(s).name: two_adic_LTE
outputs: determine $\operatorname{ord}_{2^m}(u)$ and translate $u^n \equiv u^t$ into $n \equiv t \pmod{2^{m-2}}$

technique(s).name: quadratic_reciprocity
outputs: existence of $x_0$ needed for Hensel lifting

technique(s).name: primitive_root_mod_prime_power
technique(s).purpose: rigidify exponents modulo $q^2$ and classify order-$4$ elements

technique(s).name: primitive_root_usage
technique(s).purpose: translate $A^n \equiv h^{(\ell-1)/2}$ into an exponent congruence modulo $\ell-1$

technique(s).name: gcd_based_reduction
technique(s).purpose: reduce and solve intermediate linear congruences by dividing by gcd and inverting

technique(s).name: generalized_CRT_sequential
inputs: progression for $n$ and remaining exponent congruences
technique(s).purpose: sequentially incorporate constraints to reach a single residue class

theorem(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem_coprime_moduli
theorem(s).role: existence and uniqueness of $A$ modulo $2^m p^k q^2 \ell$

theorem(s).name: lifting_the_exponent_LTE_p_equals_2
theorem(s).role: valuation formula $v_2(a^n-b^n)=v_2(a-b)+v_2(a+b)+v_2(n)-1$ for odd $a,b$ and even $n$

theorem(s).name: Hensel_lemma_simple_root
theorem(s).role: unique lifting of a simple root modulo $q$ to modulo $q^2$

theorem(s).name: cyclic_group_order_formula
theorem(s).role: order of $g^a$ in a cyclic group is $m/\gcd(m,a)$

theorem(s).name: linear_congruence_theorem
theorem(s).role: solvability and reduction of $ax \equiv b \pmod m$

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Equality of powers in a finite cyclic group is equivalent to congruence of exponents modulo the group order.

critical_trick(s): Convert each power condition on $A^n$ into a linear congruence on $n$ using LTE, 2-adic LTE, primitive-root rigidity, and an order-$4$ Hensel-lifted target.
Solve the coupled exponent system by gcd reduction and sequential CRT, then take the least positive solution and normalize modulo $999$.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: CRT construction, order checks, and solving congruence systems

task(s).name: verify_instance
task(s).reason: compute A by CRT, compute D,B,C, solve exponent congruences for n0, verify all four power congruences, and compute N=n0 mod 999
